title: The `when` keyword rendered as it appears in a Precept file — monospace font, keyword purple, terminal dark background. `when` is the guard: the conditional that makes some transition paths exist only when a specific structural condition is true.
conceptualBridge: The `when` code token means Precept's guards are structural declarations because the keyword appears in the transition rule itself — `when condition` makes a path exist only when the contract permits it.
decision: Rejected: legibility hard fail at 32px (score 2.5) — four characters at icon scale compress to an illegible word blur. The `->` operator (39) is the stronger code-aesthetic representative; keyword variants need a different approach to work at small sizes.
